title: Lucknow fire: Youngsters trapped, leap from first floor as rescuers battle flames
content: Tragedy struck Lucknow's Aliganj as a devastating fire engulfed a three-floor commercial building, claiming at least 15 lives, predominantly students at an animation coaching center. Thick smoke and flames turned the premises into a death trap, prompting desperate escape attempts and a massive rescue operation. Officials confirmed the fatalities, with many injured. The incident has sparked widespread grief and calls for improved fire safety.

title: Karnataka: 2 boys tied to school grill, beaten with slippers after cricket ball hits woman
content: Two boys, aged 14 and 12, were allegedly tied to school windows and beaten with slippers and hands in Bagalkot, Karnataka, after a cricket ball they were playing with accidentally hit a woman. Three individuals have been apprehended by police in connection with the incident, which occurred on Sunday. The injured children are currently receiving medical attention.

title: 'Don't try to change it': Sooryavanshi warned by 81-year-old cricket legend
content: Cricket legend Clive Lloyd has lauded teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, believing he has the makings of a future star.  Lloyd, who faced formidable pace attacks, suggested Sooryavanshi's skills would have been a significant challenge. He also urged for the youngster's natural playing style to be preserved.  This praise follows Sooryavanshi's record-breaking 11-ball fifty for India A, fueling anticipation for his potential international debut in Ireland.

title: WhatsApp finds its next CEO in India’s Kunal Shah
content: Meta, WhatsApp's parent company, has appointed Kunal Shah, founder of Indian fintech CRED, as the new Head of WhatsApp. Shah, who successfully scaled CRED to significant revenue and user growth, will take over from Will Cathcart, who is moving to a new role at Meta focused on future products. Meta is also investing in CRED as a minority shareholder.

title: 'Shinde has undergone Caesarean, gave birth to 6 traitors': Raut as UBT rebels join Shiv Sena
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut has sharply criticized Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde, accusing him of "giving birth to traitors" after six rebel Lok Sabha MPs joined Shinde's faction. Raut's remarks, including a reference to a "Cesarean surgery," came as Shinde declared "Operation Tiger complete" following the induction of the MPs. This marks another significant split within the Uddhav Thackeray camp.

title: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson nightclub controversy: ECB delivers final verdict
content: England stars Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been cleared of any wrongdoing following a nightclub incident after their win against New Zealand. While they received warnings and missed the second Test for breaching curfew, the Cricket Regulator found insufficient evidence for disciplinary action. Both players are expected to return for the series decider, shifting focus back to the 1-1 tied cricket series.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: 'Tragic loss': Indians among 13 killed in Qatar gas plant explosion
content: A tragic industrial accident at Qatar's Ras Laffan gas facility has claimed at least 13 lives, including Indian nationals, and injured 66. The blast at the Barzan gas supply facility, ruled an accident, occurred Sunday night. Despite the incident, Qatar's energy minister assured that domestic needs and LNG exports remain unaffected, though repairs will take time.

title: Watch: Australia players recreate Dhoni-Yuvraj's iconic bike celebration
content: Australia completed a 3-0 T20I series sweep over Bangladesh with a seven-wicket win, powered by Mitchell Marsh's 28-ball 60. Tim David sealed the chase and later celebrated with Matt Renshaw on a motorcycle, while Nathan Ellis also joined the festivities, recreating memories of MS Dhoni and Yuvraj Singh's iconic bike ride during their playing days.

title: DHS to appeal US court on order blocking $100,000 H-1B fee
content: The Department of Homeland Security is urging an appeals court to reinstate a $100,000 H-1B visa fee, arguing a judge's block harms immigration enforcement. The fee, intended to curb foreign worker replacement of Americans, was struck down by a judge citing separation of powers. DHS contends the President has authority for such measures, warning that delays allow more foreign workers entry, potentially causing irreparable damage.

title: Forged futures: How scammers are exploiting job aspirants
content: A disturbing surge in recruitment scams is preying on job seekers' dreams of government careers. Criminals are using forged documents, fake online exams, and impersonations of high-ranking officials like IAS and IPS officers to swindle aspirants. These elaborate frauds, often involving significant financial and emotional investment, highlight a growing trend of sophisticated cybercrime targeting hopeful individuals across India.

title: European mom adopts Indian parenting habits; says they transformed family life
content: A European mother of four, Ksenia Kala, shares how embracing Indian parenting traditions has enriched her family life. From co-sleeping and daily oil massages for babies to thoughtfully choosing Indian names and dressing up in sarees for birthdays, these practices have become cherished parts of her family's story. Kala emphasizes that good parenting ideas can be found anywhere, proving that cultural exchange can lead to beautiful family connections.

title: World Cup history! For the first time in the 21st century, Iran become...
content: Iran's veteran squad held Belgium to a goalless draw in a gritty World Cup encounter. Despite Belgium's dominance and numerous shots, Iran's solid defense, spearheaded by goalkeeper Beiranvand, proved impenetrable. A red card for Belgium's Ngoy in the second half further complicated their attacking efforts, leaving Group G wide open with all teams still in contention.

title: Coaching centre turns death trap: 15 students killed in Lucknow fire; UP CM visits scene
content: A devastating fire swept through a coaching-cum-gaming zone in Lucknow's Aliganj area on Monday afternoon, claiming the lives of at least 12 young students. Fourteen fire tenders battled the blaze that erupted around 3 pm. While authorities are investigating the exact origin, the incident has sent shockwaves through the city, with many others reportedly injured in the tragedy.

title: Father breaks down as daughter misses NEET re-exam; her response wins hearts
content: A father's desperate plea outside a NEET exam center in Vidisha, Madhya Pradesh, after his daughter arrived two minutes late, has gone viral. Despite traveling 70 kilometers and facing a motorcycle puncture, they were denied entry. The heartbreaking scene of the father collapsing highlights the immense pressure and shared anxiety families experience during these crucial exams, underscoring the profound parental role in a child's resilience.

title: US eases sanctions on Iranian oil exports as talks move towards final peace agreement
content: In a significant development, the US has greenlit the production, delivery, and sale of Iranian oil for a 60-day period. This move, tied to ongoing peace talks, allows for crucial transit in the Strait of Hormuz and IAEA inspections.  The temporary waiver, effective until August 21, also permits imports into the US under specific conditions, impacting global energy markets.

title: Sanju Samson name Dhoni cricket's Federer, compares Virat Kohli to...
content: Cricket star Sanju Samson drew parallels between cricketing legends and tennis icons, comparing MS Dhoni to Roger Federer for his calm demeanor and Virat Kohli to Carlos Alcaraz for his explosive style. He also reflected on India's World Cup triumphs, highlighting their impact on setting high standards.  Samson acknowledged Australia's past dominance but noted India's current champion status, while Rohan Bopanna shared his Wimbledon picks and cherished memories.

title: A decade since Brexit: How London's housing market got pushed into slow lane in the aftermath
content: London's housing market has experienced a decade of subdued growth, with a mere nine percent price increase since the Brexit vote, starkly contrasting the national 40 percent rise. Experts attribute this slowdown to prolonged political uncertainty, the pandemic, and economic headwinds. While Brexit played a role, subsequent crises like inflation and rising interest rates have also significantly impacted affordability, though London's inherent strengths are expected to ensure future resilience.

title: Lucknow fire: 15 dead in Aliganj coaching centre blaze; PM Modi condoles deaths
content: Tragedy struck Lucknow as a devastating fire ripped through a commercial complex in Aliganj, claiming at least 15 young lives. The blaze, originating in a coaching centre and gaming zone, spread rapidly, forcing desperate escapes, with some students seen leaping from the building. Authorities are investigating the cause and potential safety lapses, while the Chief Minister and Prime Minister have expressed deep sorrow and ordered swift relief efforts.

title: The new missile order: How India is changing the way it builds its precision arsenal
content: Missiles are no longer niche assets reserved for select targets. They are increasingly becoming consumable instruments of warfare, used repeatedly to suppress air defences, destroy logistics hubs, disrupt command networks and shape battlefield conditions. The challenge, therefore, is no longer limited to developing sophisticated missiles. A production system dependent on a single dominant manufacturer can struggle to meet those demands.

title: NSA Ajit Doval holds talks with Iran's top security official amid Middle East tensions
content: National Security Adviser Ajit Doval met with Iran's deputy defense secretary Ghadir Nezamipour in New Delhi, discussing the Middle East's security landscape, BRICS cooperation, and bilateral ties. This meeting occurred on the sidelines of the 16th BRICS National Security Advisers' Meeting, which India is hosting. Discussions also touched upon global challenges, terrorism, and the evolving nature of security threats.

title: 'Sachin's career almost got cut short': Ex-cricketer's warning for Sooryavanshi
content: Former South Africa batter Daryll Cullinan expresses concern over the physical toll on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive batting style. Cullinan fears for the youngster's wrists and joints, citing the increased demands of modern cricket and heavier bats. He draws parallels to Sachin Tendulkar's career-threatening elbow injury, urging attention to Sooryavanshi's long-term health as his workload grows.

title: UAE looking to buy India's BrahMos, Akashteer air defence system: Report
content: India is in advanced discussions with the UAE for a significant defence deal, potentially including the sale of BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Akashteer air defence systems. This move signifies deepening strategic ties and India's growing prowess as a defence exporter. The UAE's interest stems from a desire to bolster its military capabilities amidst regional security concerns, marking a potential major win for India's indigenous defence industry.

title: Skoda dealer took two months to repair car, court orders to pay Rs 3.25 lakh
content: A Maharashtra consumer commission has ordered Skoda and its dealer to pay Rs 3 lakh to a customer whose premium car was stuck at a service centre for 58 days due to a starting issue. The commission found both parties liable for deficiency in service, rejecting the dealer's excuse of imported spare parts.

title: UPSC CSAT under review as panel examines govt job vacancies
content: A Parliamentary Standing Committee led by BJP leader and former IPS officer Brij Lal reviewed vacancies across central government departments and assessed the progress of recruitment drives. The panel also examined the functioning of recruitment agencies such as UPSC, SSC, and DoPT, while discussing the Civil Services Examination and CSAT. The committee said recruitment efforts were progressing satisfactorily and stressed continued monitoring of vacancies.

title: As Keir Starmer announces exit, 'chief mouser' Larry the Cat stays on awaiting PM no 7
content: Amidst political upheaval and Prime Minister Keir Starmer's resignation, Larry the cat, the "chief mouser" of Downing Street, has once again captured public attention. This seasoned feline, having served six previous prime ministers, now anticipates the seventh. Humorous posts from his official parody account have lightened the mood, with Larry humorously "accepting" Starmer's resignation and commenting on the frequent leadership changes.

title: 'Maa Inti Bangaram' OTT release: When and where to watch Samantha Ruth Prabhu's film
content: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's action-comedy drama, 'Maa Inti Bangaram,' released on June 19, 2026, is a theatrical hit, drawing praise and strong box office numbers. While an official OTT release date is pending, reports suggest JioHotstar has secured the digital rights. The film, a blend of family sentiments, comedy, and thriller, is expected to stream online four to six weeks post-theatrical release, likely in late July or early August 2026.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian stock markets surged on Monday, with the Sensex and Nifty reclaiming key levels. This rebound was fueled by a dip in crude oil prices, substantial foreign fund inflows, and positive developments in US-Iran diplomatic talks. Heavyweights like Reliance and HDFC Bank led the recovery, boosting overall market sentiment and encouraging cautious optimism among investors.

title: What happens to a flat when the owner dies without a will?
content: Without a will, property inheritance in India involves intricate legalities that vary by religion. The Hindu Succession Act, Muslim Personal Law, and Indian Succession Act determine the distribution of assets among heirs. Grasping these laws, along with understanding the classifications of heirs and their shares, is vital for the efficient transfer of property. Essential documents, such as legal heir certificates and relinquishment deeds, play a pivotal role in this process.

title: Akshay Kumar starrer, 'Welcome to the Jungle' receives CBFC clearance after 18 changes
content: Ahmed Khan's hilariously anticipated film 'Welcome to the Jungle' has received a UA 16+ rating from the CBFC after undergoing 18 significant edits to meet compliance standards. With a release date set for June 26, this adventurous sequel boasts a star-studded cast, revamped dialogue, and the removal of certain visuals to ensure it resonates with audiences while keeping the thematic essence of the beloved franchise.

title: SSC CGL exam registration: Last date to apply for over 12k vacancies; check details
content: The registration window for SSC CGL 2026 will close at 11 PM on June 22, giving aspirants their final opportunity to apply for one of the country’s largest government recruitment drives. The examination aims to fill around 12,256 vacancies across central government departments. Candidates can pay the application fee until June 23, while the correction facility will remain available from June 29 to July 1.

title: How crude price crash could turn the tide for India's state-owned oil giants
content: State-run oil companies are poised for improved profits as falling crude prices boost fuel marketing margins. While recent excise duty cuts have aided this recovery, rising debt and potential future tax hikes pose risks to long-term earnings. Analysts anticipate stronger performance from the second quarter, with BPCL and IOC seen as top beneficiaries if oil prices remain subdued.

title: Royal Ascot: Where the real spectacle isn't the horses
content: For over 300 years, Royal Ascot has been one of Britain's grandest social events. But on screen, the horses are often little more than supporting cast. The real drama unfolds in the stands — in the hats, the hierarchy and the silent performance of belonging.

title: 'Speed up your calculation': Gukesh advised ahead of World Championship match
content: Ahead of his world title defence against Javokhir Sindarov, D Gukesh has been advised by Grandmaster Krishnan Sasikiran to improve his speed, technique and handling of time pressure. Sasikiran warned that Sindarov is highly prepared and fast-paced, but said Gukesh's previous world championship experience could prove crucial in what promises to be a tough contest.

title: Resort charged Rs 15 extra on a Mountain Dew bottle, court orders Rs 10k compensation
content: A Haryana resort faces a consumer commission's order to refund Rs 75 and pay Rs 10,000 in compensation. The resort overcharged a customer for Mountain Dew during a party, charging Rs 50 per bottle against a printed price of Rs 35. When the customer raised the issue, resort staff allegedly misbehaved. The commission found this a clear deficiency in service, ordering the refund with interest and compensation for harassment.

title: Andhra Pradesh strikes gold with Jonnagiri mine, eyes more projects
content: Commercial production begins at Jonnagiri in Kurnool district as the State prepares to auction several gold-bearing blocks in Chittoor and Anantapuramu to boost investment and employment

title: Sridhar Babu meets Piyush Goyal, seeks Central support for projects
content: Logistics hub in Zaheerabad, approval for plug-and-play industrial parks across the State as well as multi-modal logistics park in Manoharabad and sanction of an aero-defence corridor sought by the State government

title: Lucknow building fire: At least 15 dead, seven injured
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announces an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh from PMNRF to the kin of each deceased; injured to get ₹50,000

title: Govt. confronts legal, political imbroglio in KSCDC cashew import corruption case
content: Case comes to the fore again after Industries Secretary  A.P.M. Mohammed Hanish appears before High Court on Monday and submits an unconditional apology in a contempt petition moved by petitioner Kadakampally Manoj, who accused the government of refusing prosecution sanction in the case

title: Watch: 15 killed in Lucknow coaching centre fire; rescue operations underway
content: At least 15 people lost their lives after a massive fire broke out in a three-storey commercial building housing a coaching centre in Lucknow’s Aliganj area

title: Sudheeran writes to CM, asks him not to lower tax on low-spirit liquor
content: The former KPCC president also asks Satheesan to shelve the proposal to allow private investment in mineral mining

title: What is ammonia poisoning? | Explained
content: Five people died and many were hospitalised after an ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing plant in Tiruvallur

title: Indians among 13 dead in Qatar explosion, says Qatar's Minister
content: Mr. Al-Kaabi, who is also the  CEO of QatarEnergy, said 66 people were injured in the fire caused by the explosion at the Barzan local gas supply facility in Ras Laffan Industrial City on Sunday (June 21, 2026) evening, Doha News reported

title: Govt plans constructing new spillway at Thottappally
content: As existing spillway at Thottappally in Ambalapuzha is not completely equipped for full water discharge owing to its age

title: Eluru police rescue three sisters who fled home from Hyderabad
content: According to the police, the minor siblings had reportedly ran away from home on Sunday (June 21) after their mother chided them for continuously watching videos on the mobile phone

title: Return enumeration forms or risk losing voter status: Hyderabad District Election Officer
content: The month-long revision process, beginning June 25, will also involve polling station rationalisation and verification of voter records ahead of the publication of revised electoral rolls

title: BJP leader presents his book to Naidu
content: Union Minister of State for Mines Bupathiraju Srinivasa Varma and State Minister of Health Satya Kumar Yadav formally release the book tilted ‘Bharat Rising: In the Modi Era’

title: Use of murky water to disperse AIYF, AISF protesters: Pinarayi brings sample to Assembly
content: Leader of the Opposition says water used in water cannon was extremely dangerous and can spread even amoebic encephalitis. Home Minister says steps will be taken to ensure that such an incident does not recur

title: Allu Arjun fails to appear before court in stampede case, hearing adjourned to July 6
content: The Namapally court has directed to submit a memo explaining the reasons for Allu Arjun’s absence who had sought  to attend the hearing via video conference

title: H.A.N.U.M.A.N team rescues spotted deer from downstream waters of Somasila dam
content: Deputy Chief Minister via a social media post says as access to the distressed animal proved difficult, the rescue team mobilised a boat and carefully navigated the waters to reach it and brought it to safety

title: Andhra Pradesh govt. signs MoU with IFMR to advance Swarna Andhra 2047 Vision
content: Institute for Financial Management and Research will assist the State Planning Department in establishing mission cells dedicated to accelerating GSDP growth, undertake field studies, provide policy recommendations and suggest innovative governance practices

title: Lucknow building fire: Mother's plea, colleague’s distress call capture horror
content: The blaze erupted at the building housing an animation centre on Usha Mehta Marg, leaving 15 people dead and seven injured

title: 50% traffic fine waiver announced during July 11 Lok Adalat
content: As per a direction of the NLSA and KSLSA, the National Lok Adalat will dispose of cases pending in various courts in Dharwad district

title: Kerala HC dismisses PIL challenging free travel for women, transgender persons in KSRTC ordinary buses
content: The Court observed that there was nothing in the plea to prove that a Government Order that had been issued with regard to the free travel scheme, was illegal

title: YSRCP seeks CBI probe into death of Perupogu Kranthi Kumar
content: Party leader alleges that Kranthi Kumar’s death followed harassment by a police officer and referred to a purported selfie video recorded by the deceased

title: State urged to improve revenue buoyancy and quality of expenditure
content: Colloquium on ‘State of the Finances of Kerala and Waste Management in Urban Local Bodies’ organised by Comptroller and Auditor General of India and the Indian Council of Social Science Research

title: Artificial limb manufacturing units to come up in five teaching hospitals in A.P.
content: Centres in Vijayawada, Visakhapatnam, Ongole, Tirupati and Kurnool to provide affordable assistive devices for persons with disabilities

title: Lucknow Coaching Centre Fire: 15 Dead, PM Modi Announces Rs 2 Lakh Aid
content: Lucknow Coaching Centre Fire Live Updates: The fire, which raged for over an hour, was eventually brought under control, after which authorities recovered at least 15 bodies from the site.

title: Sikkim Electoral Roll Revision Identifies 20,000 Voters For Deletion
content: SIR of electoral rolls in Sikkim is progressing on schedule, with election authorities having distributed nearly all Enumeration Forms and digitised more than 84 per cent of the electorate's records, Chief Election Officer Raj Yadav said.

title: CBI Arrests Ex Reliance Finance CEOs In Rs 7,623 Crore Bank Fraud Case
content: "In RCFL case, the accused have caused a loss of Rs 4,097 crore to 13 Public Sector Banks (PSBs), whereas in RHFL, the loss amount is Rs 3,526 crore to 10 PSBs," a CBI spokesperson said in a statement.

title: "Wish To Return Home Soon": Kashmiri Pandits At Kheer Bhawani Mela
content: Dedicated to Goddess Ragnya Devi, the Mela is one of the largest Hindu gatherings in the valley, second only to the Amarnath Yatra.

title: Visit Not About Kashmir: Shashi Tharoor After Row Over "Encouraging" Comment
content: Shashi Tharoor's positive vibe and the "encouraging progress towards normalcy" comment did not go down well with the Congress or the political parties in the Valley.

title: Indians Among 13 Killed In Qatar Gas Plant Explosion, 66 Injured
content: The blast happened as workers were restarting operations, which were halted after an Iranian attack in March.

title: Mahrang Baloch, Prominent Baloch Activist, Sent To Life Imprisonment By Pakistan
content: Currently in custody, Mahrang Balcoh has been for years a vocal critic of the policies of the Pakistani government toward the Baloch People.

title: 'Oracle, Maestro': Former US Federal Reserve Chair Alan Greenspan Dies
content: In his 18 and a half years at the helm of the Fed, Greenspan presided over a sustained era of American growth and prosperity, yet one that ended with devastating consequences in 2008, two years after he had left the central bank.

title: Payment Dispute, Fatal Assault: Odisha Man's Death Exposes Kerala Brothel
content: Danpat Naik, a native of Odisha, died at Thrissur Medical College Hospital after he was allegedly attacked following a dispute over payment at the brothel in Korappath on June 18.

title: 'Pakistani Forces Out': PoK's Youngest Lead Protests From Front Lines
content: The protests are being coordinated by the Awami Action Committee (AAC), which has set a deadline of 23 June for the Pakistani government to accept a charter containing 38 demands.

title: Nabha Foundation Brings Punjab's Timeless Phulkari Tradition To Delhi
content: The Nabha Foundation has launched Handcrafting Heritage, a two-week exhibition celebrating the rich legacy of Punjab's iconic Phulkari embroidery tradition at Drishti Hall, The Kunj, New Delhi.

title: JD Vance Says US May Unfreeze Some Iranian Assets, Hints At Successful Deal
content: JD Vance's comments came after he and Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Monday wrapped up a lengthy round of initial talks aimed at solidifying a permanent end to the war between the countries.

title: MBBS Student Sejal Pawar Dehumanised Cadavers, Internet Did The Same To Her
content: Sejal Pawar was rightly criticised for her dehumanising remarks. Her college sent her on a 15-day forced medical leave, and Maharashtra Cyber has registered an FIR naming her. But the faceless trolls did to Pawar what she did to the male cadavers through her words - she was stripped of her dignity.

title: 'Put Myself Forward': Andy Burnham Says He Will Run For UK Prime Minister
content: Former health secretary Wes Streeting, who previously said he would join a leadership race, instead said he was backing Burnham

title: Denied Official Car, Kerala Panchayat Leader Cycles 30 Km To Attend Event
content: Edward alleged that the denial was linked to a recent dispute over a tar-mixing unit functioning in his ward.

title: 1.8 Kg Heroin, Rs 3.9 Lakh Recovered As Cops Bust Drug Network In Punjab
content: In a major crackdown on drug trafficking, Punjab's Fazilka Police has busted a narcotics supply network and recovered more than 1.8 kilograms of heroin, along with Rs 3.9 lakh suspected to be drug money. Three accused were arrested in connection with

title: Trump tore up Obama's Iran deal. His own version now faces the Hormuz & Lebanon test
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: The skeleton lake found in 1942 still holds an unsolved secret

title: Keir Starmer resigns: The British prime ministership is now a subscription service
content: a time when becoming prime minister of Britain represented the culmination of a life’s work . men spent decades climbing party ladders, surviving ideological civil wars, losing elections, winning elections, extending the empire, writing memoirs, and developing the sort of permanent expression that suggested they had personally negotiated the Treaty of Versailles . now it appears to be a two-year free trial .
